title: Align materialized state with the minimal continuation contract
plan_sections: Server-owned transaction variables vs client-managed inputs
goal: Align the runtime materialized state implementation with the minimal canonical continuation contract defined in the 0.6.0 plan so resume correctness depends only on the intended top-level fields and the no-active baseline is represented structurally and unambiguously.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 design makes materialized state the canonical resume entrypoint. If tx_state carries extra authoritative fields, mixes active and no-active semantics, or leaves baseline behavior ambiguous, later identity, resume, and compatibility work will drift from the plan. This ticket makes the materialized state shape explicit and authoritative before downstream runtime behavior is finalized.
scope: Review the code that defines, writes, updates, reads, validates, and rebuilds materialized transaction state.
Align the top-level materialized state shape to the minimal canonical continuation contract.
Ensure `active_tx` is treated as an identity-bearing active-transaction snapshot rather than a lifecycle-classification field.
Ensure top-level `status` remains the canonical lifecycle classification field.
Ensure no-active baseline representation is structural and matches the plan.
Ensure `verify_state` and `commit_state` are `null` in the canonical no-active baseline.
Ensure `last_applied_seq` is retained in the no-active baseline when available.
Ensure completed work is not exposed as resumable active work once the materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline.
Identify and demote any currently present phase-style, step-style, session-derived, or guidance-only fields that are incorrectly treated as part of the canonical continuation contract.
out_of_scope: Defining or changing the canonical checkpoint event set except where materialized state semantics require consistency.
Implementing historical compatibility normalization for event-log formats beyond what is necessary to preserve materialized-state correctness.
inputs: Any transaction-state serializers, validators, and rebuild logic under src

no_active_baseline.last_applied_seq: retains the last materialized event sequence when available

rules: Treat the listed canonical_fields as the only top-level materialized-state fields required for deterministic resume.
Treat `status` as the canonical lifecycle classification field and keep it top-level.
Treat `active_tx` as an identity-bearing active-transaction snapshot and not as the lifecycle-classification field.
Do not require phase-style, step-style, session-derived, or guidance-only fields for deterministic continuation.
When the materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline, `verify_state` and `commit_state` must be `null`.
When the materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline, completed work must not remain exposed as resumable active work.
Any retained non-canonical fields must be clearly treated as derived, optional, or compatibility-only and must not control resume correctness.
tasks: Identify the runtime structures and modules that own the materialized transaction state shape.
Map existing tx_state fields to the plan-defined canonical fields and identify extras.
Update materialized-state writing logic so the top-level state shape matches the plan.
Update materialized-state reading and validation logic so no-active baseline handling is explicit and consistent.
Ensure post-terminal transitions converge to the canonical no-active baseline without leaving resumable active state behind.
Confirm that `last_applied_seq` behavior is preserved correctly across active and no-active states.
Document or annotate any non-canonical fields that remain for compatibility or observability only.
expected_outputs: A code-level materialized-state schema aligned with the minimal canonical continuation contract.
A clear no-active baseline representation implemented consistently in runtime code.
A list of non-canonical materialized-state fields that remain and why they do not control continuation correctness.
A short alignment note describing how active state, no-active baseline, and post-terminal normalization now behave.
acceptance_criteria: The runtime materialized state can be described in terms of the canonical fields: `active_tx`, `status`, `next_action`, `verify_state`, `commit_state`, `semantic_summary`, and `last_applied_seq`.
The runtime no-active baseline matches the plan-defined structural representation.
The implementation clearly distinguishes `active_tx` from top-level `status` responsibilities.
Once materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline, `verify_state` and `commit_state` are `null`.
Once materialized state reaches the canonical no-active baseline, completed work is not exposed as resumable active work.
Optional or derived metadata is not required for continuation correctness.
Materialized state remains usable as the canonical resume entrypoint and stays consistent with the next_action-driven resume model.
review_checkpoints: Confirm that top-level lifecycle classification is represented only by `status`.
Confirm that no-active baseline handling does not rely on sentinel identifiers or reconstructed fake transactions.
Confirm that any retained compatibility or observability fields are not consulted for deterministic resume dispatch.
Confirm that post-terminal normalization cannot leave behind an apparently resumable active transaction.
notes: This ticket should be implemented after the minimal continuation contract and status-versus-next_action responsibilities are explicit in code.
Any ambiguity discovered while aligning tx_state should be resolved in favor of the plan's minimal materialized-state contract.
This ticket is the materialized-state counterpart to exact active-transaction selection and next_action-driven resume.
